steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to "begin a narrative" about Wong's award-nominated web series. A narrative opening needs to name the creator, name the series, and set the scene (when it launched and what kind of show it was) so a reader can follow what comes next.

steps.title: Find the choice that opens cleanly
steps.content: The best opener introduces Freddie Wong as the creator, identifies Video Game High School as the subject, and anchors the story in time and genre.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — In 2012, director and visual effects artist Freddie Wong launched a new action-comedy web series: Video Game High School.: gives the year, Wong's name and role, the genre, and the series title in a single launching sentence. ✓
  • Choice B — Video Game High School was celebrated for its inventive video game–centric world and high-quality special effects, and it was nominated for a Producer's Guild Award for Outstanding Digital Series.: leads with praise and the award, which is a conclusion, not a narrative beginning, and never names Wong. ✗
  • Choice C — Wong, cocreator of the YouTube channel RocketJump, would go on to see his web series be nominated for a Producers Guild Award.: leans on the RocketJump aside and never names the series, so it does not open the narrative about VGHS. ✗
  • Choice D — In 2012, Video Game High School premiered on RocketJump; it would later be nominated for an award.: omits Wong entirely and reduces the series to a vague "an award," weak narrative framing. ✗
That is the correct answer.
